  8336.  [s2_4dedb1be6aabf575028a2ed88708d7e8dbbe2db1]  Metric Entropy-Free Sample Complexity Bounds for Sample Average Approximation in Convex Stochastic ProgrammingResults (Theorems and and their related discussions) in Section of this paper extend the conference paper Liu, H., Tong, J. (2024) New Sample Complexity Bounds for Sample Average Approximation in Heavy-Tailed Stochastic Programming, in Proceedings of the 41st International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML), Vienna, Austria. PMLR 235, 2024. black In that paper, two key assumptions are imposed: (i) $f(,)$ is differentiable on $X$ for almost every $$, and (ii) an exact solution to the SAA problem can be computed. The present work relaxes both assumptions: everywhere differentiability is not always required, and our main results apply to inexact SAA solutions. Moreover, Sections , , and Appendix develop, respectively, light-tailed results, non-Lipschitz bounds, and lower sample complexity analysis that go beyond the conference version; the corresponding statements, analyses, and proofs are entirely new. Finally, the numerical experiments reported here are also not included in the ICML paper.
